zotero.abstractNote: We present OpenScene, a method for 3D scene understanding that supports open-vocabulary queries. Given a 3D scene, OpenScene learns to map 3D points to a feature space that is aligned with the feature space of a pre-trained 2D vision-language model (e.g., CLIP). This allows the model to perform a wide range of 3D scene understanding tasks, such as 3D semantic segmentation, local feature matching, and open-vocabulary 3D object detection, without requiring any task-specific training or 3D labels. We demonstrate that OpenScene achieves state-of-the-art performance on several 3D scene understanding benchmarks, while also enabling new capabilities such as querying 3D scenes with arbitrary text prompts.
zotero.conferenceName: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings 2023 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
